sanskrit_devanagari:
परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम् ।
धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय सम्भवामि युगे युगे ॥ ८ ॥
sanskrit_iast:
paritrāṇāya sādhūnāṁ vināśāya ca duṣkṛtām |
dharma-saṁsthāpanārthāya sambhavāmi yuge yuge || 8 ||

anvaya.meaning: for the purpose of firm establishment
I am born / I come forth

translations.verbatim_quote: To deliver the pious and to annihilate the miscreants, as well as to reestablish the principles of religion, I Myself appear, millennium after millennium.
To deliver the pious and to annihilate the miscreants, as well as to reestablish the principles of religion, I Myself appear, millennium after millennium.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 3590 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: According to Bhagavad-gītā, a sādhu (holy man) is a man in Kṛṣṇa consciousness. A person may appear to be irreligious, but if he has the qualifications of Kṛṣṇa consciousness wholly and fully, he is to be understood to be a sādhu.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 4.8 Paritranaya, for the protection; sadhunam, of the pious, the followers of the virtuous path; vinasaya, for the destruction; duskrtam, of the evil-doers, of the sinful ones; and also dharmasamsthapanarthaya, for establishing virtue fully;-for that purpose, sambhavami, I manifest Myself; yuge yuge…
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-4-8-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Adi Shankaracharya of Advaita Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Shankara reads the verse as the explicit purpose-statement of incarnation: protection of the dharma-keepers, destruction of the dharma-violators, and the firm reinstatement of dharma itself. All three are aimed at preserving the conditions under which liberation remains accessible.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 4.8 The good are those who follow the Dharma, as defined above. They are the foremost among the Vaisnavas, who have taken refuge in Me. While My name, acts and form are inaccessible to speech and thought, these devotees cannot get support, sustenance etc., for themselves without perceiving Me.
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-4-8-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Ramanuja of Sri Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Ramanuja reads the verse as expanding 4.7's mechanism into purpose. The Lord's incarnation is not punitive in any narrow sense; the destruction of the wicked is itself a deliverance for them (their adharma is dissolved by encounter with the divine), and the protection of the saintly is the preservation of those whose lives sustain the dharma. The repeated yuge yuge marks the regularity, not the rarity.

positions.rendering: destruction of the dharma-violators, aimed at restoring the conditions for liberation
the destruction is itself a deliverance — encounter with the divine dissolves the duṣkṛta's adharma

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: The fault-line is whether 'destruction' is read narrowly (punishment) or broadly (transformation by divine encounter). Ramanuja's compassionate reading is distinctive: the wicked are not merely punished, they are released. The Vishishtadvaita reading is theologically closer to a soteriology of grace. Engineering layers must not flatten this verse into 'remove bad actors'; the Sanskrit allows multiple readings.

literal_meaning: For the protection of the good, the destruction of the wicked, and the firm establishment of dharma — I come forth age after age.
traditional_meaning_consensus: The threefold purpose of the avatar: (1) paritrāṇāya sādhūnām — protection of the virtuous, (2) vināśāya duṣkṛtām — destruction of the wicked, (3) dharma-saṁsthāpanārtha — re-establishment of dharma. The verse is the most quoted in popular religious culture. Yuge yuge marks recurrence: this is not a one-time intervention but a cyclical pattern across cosmic ages.
